stance: Adopt option B as the working baseline; treat option A as a fallback only if the integration test fails.
recommendation: Pick option B and ship a thin vertical slice end-to-end before optimizing.
reasoning: Option B has fewer moving parts, which shortens the feedback loop on the contract assumptions.
Round 1 surfaced no blocking risks unique to option B that option A also avoids.
A vertical slice exposes integration mismatches earlier than a horizontal layer-by-layer build.
objections: Option B couples the data layer to the transport, which would slow a future swap of the transport.
The vertical slice approach defers performance work that may be hard to retrofit.
risks: If the contract changes after the slice ships, downstream consumers need to re-integrate.
Concurrency limits on the shared dependency may bottleneck the slice under realistic load.
changesFromPriorRound: Dropped the multi-backend abstraction from MVP scope after round 1 flagged it as premature.
Replaced the staged rollout proposal with a single-cutover plan to reduce coordination cost.
openQuestions: Should the slice include observability hooks, or is logging sufficient for the first cut?
What is the rollback plan if the slice exposes a contract issue after release?
